description_long: This is a two-story brick and stone house with a steep gable roof and a single chimney on one side. The front facade features a symmetrical arrangement of four windows with white trim and a central entry door, which is accessed by a small set of steps. The lower portion of the front wall is clad in stone, while the upper portion and sides are constructed from red brick. The windows are covered with curtains, and the door has a decorative glass panel. The roof appears to be made of dark shingles and shows some weathering. The overall design is simple and functional, typical of early 20th-century urban residential architecture.
description: A two-story brick and stone house with a gable roof, central entry door, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stone and brick facade, four windows, central door with steps, and chimney visible on the left.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain red brick wall with the chimney and the gable roof's slope.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front but with all-brick facade, four windows, and the chimney on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain red brick wall and the gable roof, chimney visible at the top.
attribute_entryway_design: Central door with small steps, decorative glass panel, no porch or overhang
